response.justification: James Smith is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and platform developer. His C++ exposure is incidental and dated — a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE, and a bachelor thesis project using C++ for audio synthesis. The core requirements of the role — deep C++/STL proficiency, multi-threaded systems, industrial IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and IEC 62443 secure development practices — are entirely absent from his profile. His distributed systems and event-driven architecture experience is real but built on web-native stacks unrelated to industrial automation.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems work at scale (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Solid Agile engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background with some C++ and low-level systems exposure, showing capacity to work outside web stacks
concerns: C++ is marginal and stale — last substantive use was 2014–2015; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of modern C++, STL, or concurrent/multi-threaded systems design
No industrial automation, embedded, or IoT domain knowledge; no exposure to MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, or IEC 62443 security standards
Educational background (BS Computer Science + Fine Arts) does not meet the B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech engineering degree requirement specified for the role
